text:
The Copper Wall Account 🧱➡️💡

My thesis: The next wave of AI infrastructure is the shift from copper interconnects to silicon photonics and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) in data centers. Power efficiency and bandwidth demands are forcing the change and I’m adding exposure to quantum computing.

Here’s my initial DCA plan:

Starting tomorrow: $5/week into each of these 10 stocks 

$TSM $AVGO $MRVL $LITE $AAOI $CRDO $FN $TSEM $IONQ $HON

•  Photonics/AI infrastructure play: TSM, AVGO, MRVL, LITE, AAOI, CRDO, FN, TSEM

•  Quantum long-shot: IONQ + HON (via Quantinuum)

I’ll post weekly updates on performance, news, and any adjustments.

If the AI buildout accelerates and silicon photonics/CPO gets widely adopted, I expect strong returns by 2030.

If IONQ or Quantinuum leads in quantum, this 10-year DCA could compound beautifully by 2040.

Who’s in on the next tech wave? Drop your thoughts 👇

text:
This is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. 

L1: Materials & Substrates

The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists.
L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics.

Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR

Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837%

InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. 

L2: Active Photonic Devices

This is where light gets made.
Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle.

Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history.

L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs

The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds.

TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint.

Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV

Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361%

Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP.

L4: PIC Platform & IP

The brain of the photonic system.
Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade.

Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte)

Top performer: Innolight +948%

Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half.

L5: Foundry & Process Platform

You need a fab. Not just any fab.
SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally.

Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM

Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542%

SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026.

L6A: Engine & Module

Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules.
Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely.

Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists.

L6B: Infrastructure

Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster.
Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but.

Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF

Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748%

Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic.

L6C: Transport

Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber.
As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down.

Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO

Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777%

WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe

L7: Test & Qualification

The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping.

Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer.

Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU

Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954%

Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives.

Great job on putting it together  @damnang2

text:
As of April 23, 2026

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure sector is showing a sharply bifurcated tape today, with large-cap AI infrastructure names holding green while speculative small and micro-cap photonics names unwind hard, the top loser being $IQE down 20.53%. The move reflects a parabolic blow-off on the London line (IQE.L ran over 107% in the two weeks into April 17 before rolling over), with the OTC mirror IQEPF gapping down today on profit-taking and zero fresh fundamental news into the May 20 earnings print (https://t.co/udYbhT79LN).

The quality leadership is sending a clear signal: capital is rotating out of speculative substrate and early-stage PIC names into the picks-and-shovels plays with visible AI capex pull-through. $MRVL leads at +5.24% on the Amazon-Anthropic $100B deal tailwind layered on top of the Google custom AI chip talks and Nvidia's $2B NVLink Fusion investment, which together anchor Marvell as the default optical DSP and custom ASIC partner across every hyperscaler (https://t.co/2jwCYbswdK).

$MRVL: 5.24%
$VIAV: 4.15%
$TER: 4.10%
$CIEN: 3.38%
$KEYS: 2.23%
$GFS: 1.90%
$FORM: 1.50%
$GLW: 0.44%
$BESIY: -0.21%
$SPX: -0.41%
$NDX: -0.57%
$VICR: -1.84%
$AEHR: -1.90%
$ATEYY: -2.01%
$TSEM: -2.34%
$LITE: -3.06%
$COHR: -3.65%
$SLOIF: -6.97%
$SIVEF: -7.78%
$AAOI: -7.82%
$POET: -8.22%
$LWLG: -10.29%
$AXTI: -13.42%
$XNDU: -16.98%
$IQE: -20.53%

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
$CIEN, $GLW: The large-cap optical layer held up on AI fabric demand, with Ciena catching a bid as 800G and 1.6T buildouts continue into 2H26 and Corning benefiting from the optical cable pull-through.
$LITE, $COHR: Both names gave back modestly after a multi-week run, looking like position-trimming rather than thesis breakdown ahead of earnings season.
$AAOI: Down 7.82%, this is the sharpest move among the transceiver names and reads as profit-taking on the recent domestic-manufacturing tariff-exemption bid plus microcap beta to the broader sector unwind.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
$GFS, $TSEM: Foundries split, with GlobalFoundries up on SiPho and advanced node traction while Tower gave back some of its recent run on sector risk-off.
$AXTI, $IQE, $SLOIF, $SIVEF: This is where the damage concentrated. AXTI down 13.42%, IQE down 20.53%, Soitec down 6.97%, SiTime down 7.78%. InP substrate demand remains the bottleneck thesis intact, but the OTC liquidity and parabolic-unwind dynamics are doing the driving today, not any fundamental deterioration.
$BESIY: Flat on the day, advanced packaging and hybrid bonding exposure providing some ballast.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
$POET, $LWLG, $XNDU: All three got hit hard (POET -8.22%, LWLG -10.29%, XNDU -16.98%) in what looks like a coordinated speculative unwind. Cramer's measured POET comments on CNBC this morning didn't help sentiment on the name, and the broader early-stage PIC basket tends to move as one when risk comes off. Thesis remains long-dated optionality, but the tape is punishing anything that ran too far too fast.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure
$TER, $KEYS, $FORM, $VIAV: The test and measurement complex was the cleanest trade on the board today, with Teradyne, Keysight, FormFactor, and Viavi all green on the read-through from strong AI infrastructure capex guidance. This is where the sector's earnings visibility is most concrete.
$MRVL, $ATEYY: Marvell up 5.24% as the clear AI infrastructure beneficiary, while Advantest gave back a bit on what appears to be profit-taking after a strong run.
$AEHR, $VICR: Both down modestly, with AEHR's move tracking the broader SiC and compound semi test cycle concern rather than company-specific news.

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
$CIEN, $GLW: The large-cap optical layer held up on AI fabric demand, with Ciena catching a bid as 800G and 1.6T buildouts continue into 2H26 and Corning benefiting from the optical cable pull-through.
$LITE, $COHR: Both names gave back modestly after a multi-week run, looking like position-trimming rather than thesis breakdown ahead of earnings season.
$AAOI: Down 7.82%, this is the sharpest move among the transceiver names and reads as profit-taking on the recent domestic-manufacturing tariff-exemption bid plus microcap beta to the broader sector unwind.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
$GFS, $TSEM: Foundries split, with GlobalFoundries up on SiPho and advanced node traction while Tower gave back some of its recent run on sector risk-off.
$AXTI, $IQE, $SLOIF, $SIVEF: This is where the damage concentrated. AXTI down 13.42%, IQE down 20.53%, Soitec down 6.97%, SiTime down 7.78%. InP substrate demand remains the bottleneck thesis intact, but the OTC liquidity and parabolic-unwind dynamics are doing the driving today, not any fundamental deterioration.
$BESIY: Flat on the day, advanced packaging and hybrid bonding exposure providing some ballast.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
$POET, $LWLG, $XNDU: All three got hit hard (POET -8.22%, LWLG -10.29%, XNDU -16.98%) in what looks like a coordinated speculative unwind. Cramer's measured POET comments on CNBC this morning didn't help sentiment on the name, and the broader early-stage PIC basket tends to move as one when risk comes off. Thesis remains long-dated optionality, but the tape is punishing anything that ran too far too fast.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure
$TER, $KEYS, $FORM, $VIAV: The test and measurement complex was the cleanest trade on the board today, with Teradyne, Keysight, FormFactor, and Viavi all green on the read-through from strong AI infrastructure capex guidance. This is where the sector's earnings visibility is most concrete.
$MRVL, $ATEYY: Marvell up 5.24% as the clear AI infrastructure beneficiary, while Advantest gave back a bit on what appears to be profit-taking after a strong run.

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure complex went risk-on across the board, led by a 10%+ rip in $SLOIF after Bloomberg crowned Soitec the best-performing European stock of 2026, up more than 300% YTD with an 84% gain this month alone , and Oddo BHF raised its target to €85 from €50 (https://t.co/EbZP37PIaz). SPX +1.20% and NDX +1.29% gave the tape a clean risk-on backdrop.
Photonics has moved from optionality to consensus theme. Hyperscaler capex is flowing through transceivers into PICs and the upstream substrate layer, and the market is now hunting picks-and-shovels exposure with structural moats. Today's laggards are mostly OTC liquidity friction or single-name overhang, not thesis breaks.

1. Compound Semi Substrates & Foundries
SLOIF, IQE, AXTI, SIVEF: The substrate layer is where the photonics money is being priced. SLOIF parabolic on Bloomberg coverage and the Oddo target hike. IQE down 11% on an insider sale headline, with Andrew Nelson dumping roughly £1.5M of stock at £0.49 per share, the biggest IQE insider sale in a year Simply Wall St, classic profit-taking on the YTD leader. AXTI continues to act as the cleaner US-listed InP proxy. SIVEF whipped on OTC liquidity, not fundamentals.
GFS, TSEM: Foundry leverage to the photonics buildout. TSEM is the silicon photonics workhorse for Tower's CPO ramp, GFS riding the broader AI custom silicon wave.

2. Optical Transceivers, DSP Silicon & Active Networking
COHR, CIEN, AAOI, LITE, MRVL: 800G/1.6T transceiver demand still the cleanest hyperscaler read-through. COHR leading today, MRVL strong on merchant DSP silicon read-through, AAOI holding bid on the 1.6T order book, LITE quiet after the Nvidia investment digestion phase.
GLW, VIAV: GLW slightly red on profit-taking after its run. VIAV ripping +7% as network test demand benefits from the same buildout.

3. Equipment, Test & Packaging
FORM, AEHR, TER, KEYS: Wafer-level test and burn-in picks-and-shovels trade. FORM and AEHR both up sharp on continued read-through from HBM and AI accelerator volume. TER and KEYS round out the test exposure.
ATEYY, BESIY: ATEYY (Aixtron) is the MOCVD tool play for compound semi growth, shares more than doubled in 2026. BESIY is the hybrid bonding leverage to advanced packaging and CPO.

4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
LWLG: Modest green on EO polymer optionality, still pre-revenue, sentiment-driven.
POET, XNDU: Both red. POET digesting after recent rally. text:
Photonic computing for edge AI is one of those stories that sounds niche until you think about energy.

If NeuraWave delivers faster inference with lower power draw, that matters anywhere AI has to live outside giant data centres.

#EdgeAI #Photonics #AIHardware

Anders Storm’s repeated, specific praise is a strong and credible counter to blanket “pumper” accusations against Serenity (@aleabitoreddit). @stormdirac 

Storm is not a random retail account: He was CEO of Sivers for 8–10 years (built the photonics side). He knows the tech, partnerships (Ayar Labs, Jabil, etc.), and execution realities better than almost anyone outside the current management. 

He’s not hyping blindly - he engages with Serenity’s threads on supply-chain mapping and calls them “great deep research.”

text:
$POET is starting to move — +75.9% in just 3 days, now pushing ~$12.8.

This isn’t random. Photonics is becoming a key bottleneck in AI infrastructure: faster data transfer, lower power, and critical for next-gen data centers.

If compute was the first wave, photonics could be next.

Price action is starting to reflect it.

$LITE $COHR $AXTI $AAOI 

#stocks #AI #photonics #semiconductors #investing

text:
Marvell $MRVL Announced they will be acquiring Polariton Technologies 

They plan to add high speed low power plasmonics based silicon photonics devices to its portfolio

This gives Marvell a stronger presence in optical technology and positions them better for next gen connectivity

This partnership plans to integrate Marvell into Nvidia’s AI Factory 

They also plan to integrate Marvell into AI-RAN ecosystem with NVLink fusion!

The partnership also includes joint development of silicon photonics https://t.co/7lhuDMAxHN

text:
$poet and $sivef indirect benefits to this $mrvl acquisition-

$poet Technologies (POET) sees indirect positive momentum, not a direct tie to Polariton itself.POET’s core tech is its Optical Interposer platform—a wafer-scale, passive-alignment hybrid photonics solution that integrates light sources, modulators, waveguides, etc., into compact optical engines/light engines. It’s upstream in the same AI interconnect value chain Marvell is aggressively building out.
Marvell’s prior Celestial AI acquisition (Dec 2025) already made POET a beneficiary: Celestial used POET’s tech (e.g., Blazar light sources) as a key enabler for its Photonic Fabric. Marvell inherited that supply relationship, and POET has confirmed purchase orders/shipments tied to it.

This new Polariton deal further validates Marvell’s heavy commitment to scaling photonics for AI (now adding plasmonic modulation on top of SiPh). It doesn’t displace POET—it reinforces the ecosystem demand for integrated optical engines. Analysts and investors have repeatedly called out POET as a “real winner” or potential M&A target in the wake of Marvell’s optics moves, as hyperscalers/NVIDIA/Marvell need proven, scalable integration platforms. 

$POET gets another leg of validation and potential order acceleration. It’s not a direct “Polariton synergy” play, but the Marvell optics flywheel helps POET’s positioning in the same AI connectivity wave.

 $sivef is a strong indirect beneficiary and one of the more credible pure-plays in the exact layer this deal targets.Sivers Photonics specializes in high-performance DFB laser arrays, CW (continuous-wave) lasers, and external light source modules explicitly designed for AI data-center optical interconnects, CPO, pluggables, and SiPh integration.
They have established partnerships in the ecosystem (e.g., with POET for light engines, Ayar Labs, and paths into Marvell/Celestial/NVIDIA optics roadmaps via the CW-WDM MSA and related collaborations). Their lasers are a critical “fuel” for SiPh-based modulators and engines—like the plasmonic + SiPh solutions Marvell just acquired.
This deal accelerates adoption of exactly the architectures Sivers supplies (1.6T+ LRO transceivers, CPO, ultra-low-power AI interconnects). Recent Sivers news (e.g., Jabil collaboration on 1.6T modules) already shows momentum; Marvell’s move pours more fuel on the fire for laser array demand.

text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries 

定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂

坐标系:
光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。

硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。

硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。

公司背景:
美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。

2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。

加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。

技术路线:
纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。

GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。

赛道位置:
硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。

Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。

它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。

财务和公司状态:
市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。

硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。

主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。

所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。

AI 周期里的角色:
"硅光代工"的稀缺标的。

想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。

核心赌注:
硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量;
GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额;
主体代工业务不继续恶化。

仓位定位:
硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。

和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。

适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。

关键观察点:
AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → 
Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 
主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。

硅光的卖铲人。

硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。 硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。 公司背景: 美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。 2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。 加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。 技术路线: 纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。 GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。 赛道位置: 硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。 Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。 它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。 财务和公司状态: 市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。 硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。 主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。 所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。 AI 周期里的角色: "硅光代工"的稀缺标的。 想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。 核心赌注: 硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量; GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额; 主体代工业务不继续恶化。 仓位定位: 硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。 和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。 适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。 关键观察点: AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。 硅光的卖铲人。"}}}, "rest_id": "2047074469135655314", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "128", text: TXN showing signs that Analog Chip Season may finally be taking off. I’ve been talking about this coming transition in the hardware trade since Q4 of last year and it finally seems like it may be upon us. The first screenshot below hurts my soul, me pumping Photonics in early December but not longing any of them, but that’s the macro tourist game for ya. Regardless, this signals the continuing evolution of the manufacturing boom going on globally. In a time where energy constraints are picking up, any hardware that can bypass those constraints will get bid up here big time, and that is exactly what Analog chips provide, energy efficiency. Listen I know I’ve been banging the drum on potential trouble coming to the AI Datacenter Buildout but I don’t think it is going to break yet or crash the economy yet. As long as NVDA remains elevated, even if flat, capital from that sector will continue to slosh around into different ancillary corners of the same trade. I.E. as NVDA has languished since Q3 of last year, memory stocks have been on a tear. I think we are going to see now a rotation from this existing capital base start going into the Analog semiconductor names. I think as manufacturing output begins to diversify away from AI, I think having exposure to Analog Semis will help buttress any pain from the AI Hardware trade that should come, once demand destruction starts getting actualized. I think I’m going to finally get some exposure to them as I think it is a great asymmetric hedge to my Downstream AI thesis being expressed thru META RDDT & IBM calls.

text: Marvell $MRVL is going strong into Photonics 🧐 Looks to me they want to control as much as possible of the supply chain (remem. my thesis about the Marvell - $POET deal, that perhaps it's only interested in the Interposer System, NOT in the POET Transceivers ==> no $SIVE Lasers)

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$SIVEF

System is layered with 4 buy and sell signals using different methods to ensure no move is missed, and also to show the confidence of the correct move.

HI CERTAINTY SELL:
any shape on top of candle (♦️, 🟪, ◼️, regardless of color)= sell

HI CERTAINLY BUY:
any shape on bottom of candle (🟩 , 🔹, ◼️, regardless of color) = buy

MEDIUM RISK BUY:
. green star under candle. Mostly accurate. Java haven’t been able to code my brain - to invalidate green star under certain conditions. I will have to manually call those green stars until Java gets it.

. ‘B’ under a candle: has capitulated, tentatively confirmed to be trending up, yet might still be invalidated later. Keep an eye on it if did buy

EARLY WARNING/EARLY WATCH:
⭐️ white star on top of candle = watch for potential sell signal

⭐️ white star on bottom of candle = watch for potential buy signal. Do not buy on 1st, 2nd stars. 3rd star could be a buy, but better to wait for high certainty buy signals

EXPLANING THE FORCE BEHIND THE MOVE UP:
⬇️ bright green arrow down on top = Smart Money support

 CHAOS ALERT:
⬇️⬇️⬇️ cluster of white arrows = big move coming, up or down

TREND DIRECTION:
🟦 blue candle = uptrend
🟨 yellow = downtrend 

BAND:
📈 above midline = bullish 
📉 below midline = bearish

text:
Which earnings are you looking forward to most this week?

We’re getting major updates across several key trends:
1. Photonics/Networks: $GLW, $CLS, $APH
2. Memory: $STX, $SNDK, $WDC
3. Grid/Energy: $BE, $XOM, $CVX
4. Finance: $HOOD, $SOFI
5. Magnificent 7: $META, $AMZN, $GOOGL, $MSFT, $AAPL
6. AI Software: $ZETA, $LMND

Very exciting week ahead. 

If Trump doesn’t do anything out of the box, it looks like we’re in for a really strong week for the S&P 500.

I’ll be paying especially close attention to sentiment in the statements, execution, forward guidance, and industry insights - particularly in Memory, Photonics, and Energy. 

My top focus: $SNDK, $GLW, and $BE.

Some analysts are calling for 40% revenue growth at $ZETA - really curious to see if they deliver.

On $HOOD and $SOFI, I’m not expecting extraordinary results. Robinhood in particular remains heavily tied to crypto market volatility and success which has recently been low.

As for the Mag 7, five of them report this week - crazy!

I’ll be watching cloud revenue growth closely at $AMZN, $GOOG, and $MSFT. 

For $META, I want updates on AI applications across their business segments, AI project progress, and ad spend trends. 

For $AAPL, I’m looking for order numbers on their recent product launches plus any fresh details on AI projects and integrations.

text:
Amkor $AMKR is currently playing out a similar pattern to many other photonics/optics/AI supply chain companies; the Bull Flag breakout!

After months of consolidation we’ve seen a huge breakout over the last few weeks, but where do we go from here?

$AMKR is currently up about 98% YTD and is looking stretched now on almost all timeframes.

Short term; $AMKR likely needs to consolidate for a few weeks, to cool down, allow the moving averages to catch up, for institutions to accumulate more shares and to build momentum for another move up.

Fundamentally; $AMKR is the go-to advanced packaging supplier for both $AVGO and $TSMC - if Amkor can continue scaling production, expect this to continue to feed through into revenues (and profits) and further long term growth.

If $AMKR can continue to execute, their stock will continue to climb long term.

The photonics report that began our initiation of the sector. 

Leading to our equity reports on $SIVE and $POET so far, with many more to come. 

Report hits on $LITE quite often.

It gives you three clear discovery lenses, CPO‑dominant clusters, OCS‑flattened fabrics, and RF‑photonics edge/SatCom.

Then, links each to specific public and private “probe” names.

Extremely in depth, report is located below.

CPO‑dominant clusters( $NVDA/ $AVGO/ $TSMC/ $LITE, $MRVL ‑Celestial),

text:
Light Speed: Why Photonics Is the Invisible Backbone of AI

KEY HIGHLIGHT

- The Marvell $MRVL + Inphi angle is the one most people underestimate. DSP chips are to optical transceivers what the engine is to a car — you cannot ship a high-speed optical module without one, and Marvell owns that market position. Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver deployed in an AI cluster is a Marvell DSP royalty.

- Coherent $COHR is the most comprehensive pure-play but also the most complex story — the II-VI/Finisar merger created integration challenges and a leveraged balance sheet. The thesis is right but execution risk is real. Worth sizing accordingly.

- Fabrinet $FN is the sleeper that rarely gets discussed. They are the contract manufacturer for most of the photonics industry, which means they get volume leverage without technology concentration risk. When the whole industry is growing, Fabrinet grows with all of it.

- Credo $CRDO is the highest-beta, most speculative name on the list — but also the one with the most potential to surprise to the upside if the AI networking upgrade cycle accelerates faster than expected.

- And the private company to watch most closely is Ayar Labs — if optical chiplets become the standard interface for next-gen AI processors, whoever owns that technology gets acquired at a price that will seem obvious in retrospect.

DSP chips are to optical transceivers what the engine is to a car — you cannot ship a high-speed optical module without one, and Marvell owns that market position. Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver deployed in an AI cluster is a Marvell DSP royalty. - Coherent $COHR is the most comprehensive pure-play but also the most complex story — the II-VI/Finisar merger created integration challenges and a leveraged balance sheet. The thesis is right but execution risk is real. Worth sizing accordingly. - Fabrinet $FN is the sleeper that rarely gets discussed. They are the contract manufacturer for most of the photonics industry, which means they get volume leverage without technology concentration risk. When the whole industry is growing, Fabrinet grows with all of it. - Credo $CRDO is the highest-beta, most speculative name on the list — but also the one with the most potential to surprise to the upside if the AI networking upgrade cycle accelerates faster than expected. - And the private company to watch most closely is Ayar Labs — if optical chiplets become the standard interface for next-gen AI processors, whoever owns that technology gets acquired at a price that will seem obvious in retrospect. Yes, that's exactly what aleabitoreddit is saying.

Photonics is in a full supercycle (started ~H1/H2 2025). Some leaders like $LITE & $COHR already ran 500-1000% so you're a bit late there (though they see 50%+ more upside possible).

Remember, I lived next door to a confirmed robotics and photonics expert, Mr. Pierrick Vulliez. This should not be difficult to understand—even for Grok.

In the footage, the hand movements of the man standing in the trench align point-for-point with the robot digger’s shovel movements. This is one of several videos I’ve posted over the past few days suggesting how closely a human operator must be controlling the machine, and all involve precise hand or head movements by the controller from what I’ve seen.

If the trench activity were human controlling another human that would imply a level of coordination I’m not prepared to interpret beyond what my current evidence supports.

text:
The recent strong rally in Credo Technology (CRDO) was sparked by its April 14 announcement acquiring DustPhotonics for ~$1.2B (cash + stock + earnouts). This adds silicon photonics tech for 800G/1.6T+ optical transceivers, expanding beyond active electrical cables into faster AI data center interconnects—targeting $500M+ optical revenue by FY27.

It built on Jefferies' fresh Buy rating, explosive AI-driven growth (200%+ YoY revenue), and bullish order flow signals. Stock up ~46% in the past 5 days as of April 23.

@Thomas_james_1 $LITE near $850 vs $SIVE at $3 — valuation divergence in photonics: fundamentals or market cap effects? Small-cap photonics names offer higher beta in upcycles but with higher volatility — option spreads may work better than outright shares.

STMicroelectronics started mass production of silicon photonics chips for AI data centers in Q1 2026 and expanded its 800-volt DC power portfolio alongside NVIDIA, while deepening ties with AWS to strengthen its position in AI infrastructure.

The company completed its acquisition of NXP's MEMS business, expected to contribute mid-range revenue in the $40 million area over time, with particular strength in automotive and industrial sensors.

In China, STM began series production of its STM32 H7 microcontroller family, with plans to extend to H5 and C5 variants, improving supply chain resilience against geopolitical pressure.The momentum came against headwinds in Q4 2025. A net loss of $30 million, impairment charges and restructuring costs totaling $141 million, and a 31.6% revenue decline in the power and discrete segment weighed heavily on results.

Management is budgeting $2 to $2.2 billion in capital expenditure for 2026, mostly directed toward SiC and 300-millimeter capacity expansion.The company sees normalization of customer inventory levels by the end of Q2 2026 as the critical threshold for sustainable recovery.

text:
THE OPTICAL PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK...🧐

​As AI clusters blast past the physical limits of copper interconnects, the bottleneck is shifting hard into high-speed optics.

​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack:

$AAOI - Transceiver Layer

Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business.

$CRDO - Connectivity Layer

Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. 
The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify.

$LITE - Laser & Switching

Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. 
Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028.

$COHR - InP Infrastructure

Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. 
Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking.

$CIEN - Transport Layer

The backbone that ties everything together. 
Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels.

$FN - Manufacturing Backbone

The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners.

$MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics

Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. 
The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design.

$AEHR - Testing & Reliability

Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). 
Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability).

The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬
Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. NFA.

high-speed optics. ​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack: $AAOI - Transceiver Layer Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business. $CRDO - Connectivity Layer Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify. $LITE - Laser & Switching Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028. $COHR - InP Infrastructure Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking. $CIEN - Transport Layer The backbone that ties everything together. Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels. $FN - Manufacturing Backbone The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners. $MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design. $AEHR - Testing & Reliability Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability). The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬 Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. text:
"The deal adds plasmonics-based silicon photonics tech that could enable faster & more power-efficient optical interconnects for next-gen AI networking."

This looks very promising. but I don't think people know what the weak point is.

text:
$MRVL just made a move in the AI infrastructure race, acquiring Polariton Technologies to lock down the shift to 3.2T optical connectivity. 

​Why this is a big deal:
• ​Adds ultra-fast, low-power 
  plasmonics-based silicon photonics 
  to their stack.
• ​Solves the massive bandwidth and 
   power bottlenecks in next-gen AI

Here are 15 names positioned for it: 1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand. 2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion. 3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity. 4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales. 5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity. 6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand. 7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers. 8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure. 9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules. 10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production. 11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers. 12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient. 13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand. 14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems. 15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster. text:
The 7-Layer Photonics Stack

1.Materials & Wafers — InP, GaAs, silicon substrates. InP supply is the chokepoint.

https://t.co/X8pBSexGF9 & Epitaxy — Equipment to grow the crystal layers. MOCVD machines. Few players.

3.Lasers & Devices — VCSELs, DFB lasers, modulators. Where the photons actually get made.

4.Foundries (III-V Fabs) — The fabs that process compound semiconductors. Scarce and specialized.

5.Test & Inspection — Verify everything works at speed and scale. Lower binary risk, often overlooked.

6.Optics & Modules — Transceivers, MEMS, co-packaged optics (CPO). Where hyperscaler demand hits first.

7.Networking & Connectivity — Optical switches, DSPs, interconnect. The system layer.

text:
Ich habe die Liste geklaut, aber einfach um sicher zu gehen, dass auch in der deutschen Bubble ein paar Leute vom Thema #Photonic mitbekommen.

THE 7 LAYERS OF PHOTONICS

1. Materials & Wafers (substrate layer)
$AXTI, $IQE $WOLF, $COHR, $LWLG

2. Tools (fabrication layer)
$ASML, $AMAT, $LRCX 

3. Lasers (light generation layer)
$LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SMTC 

4. Foundries (manufacturing layer)
$TSM, $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $INTC

5. Test, Inspection & Packaging (reliability layer)
$AEHR, $VIAV, $ONTO, $AMKR, $FN

6. Optics (module layer)
$AAOI, $POET, $GLW

7. Networking (connectivity layer)
$CRDO, $MRVL, $AVGO, $ANET, $CIEN

text:
c:7

$M7U (Nynomic AG) is a German micro-cap, off the beaten path, directly part of the photonics supply chain.

Here’s my simplified explanation of what this company does and why I like it:

AI datacenters need massive amounts of fiber optic connections to move data between chips at the speed of light. Those fiber optic components are manufactured inside specialized reactors made by a company called AIXTRON – which controls roughly 90% of the global market for these machines.

Nynomic owns LayTec – a company that makes the sensors that sit inside every single one of those AIXA reactors and measure what’s happening in real time during production. Without them, manufacturing yields drop dramatically. Near 1:1 attach rate on every reactor AIXA ships.

AIXA’s optics segment is their primary growth engine for 2026, guided at €520M revenue. LayTec rides that wave on every single order.

Now the valuation gap compared to its nearest competitors. $ONTO trades at ~12.7x sales. $CAMT trades at ~15x sales. Nynomic trades at ~1.5x sales. 

The market is currently treating it like a struggling industrial company because of the 2025 restructuring noise – but management guides for significant recovery in 2026 with disproportionately large profit expansion. Medium-term target is €200M revenue at 16-19% margins.

Given the current market cap, hitting those targets would likely force a massive re-rating toward the 3x–5x sales range typical for European specialized semi-cap players.

You’re essentially buying a hidden monopoly inside the hottest semiconductor supply chain in the world at a steep discount – before it has had a chance to run.

This is a position I opened on $IBKR last week. Not available on $HOOD. Looking to add more this week if the price dips or stays flat a little longer.

text: Seeing $TSEM going Parabolic is what got me so obsessed with $GFS doing the same thing, until I started reading how GloFo's photonics aren't exactly up to par with Towers, and realized why the stock has been so badly shorted 😵 Anyway, they are starting to catch up, and $AMD is using $GFS for the their MI500 GPUs! If I'm right, GlobalFoundries will hit $100 by early November where my Finger Icon is pointing, when it intersects the 3/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's July'22 low, and the 1/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's April'25 low.   $GFS is shorted to the gills with 40% of the float short, the photonics bonanza could definitely send this sucker to $100 in frenzy, bookmarking this post in case it turns out as predicted.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
光コンピュータって聞いたことある？ボクがいま注目してるのは、オックスフォード大学発祥のLumaiという企業なんだ。

ElectronicsじゃなくPhotonics（光）でAIチップの課題を解く。通常のGPUは電子で計算するけど、光は速度が違うんだよ。消費電力も少なくて済む。

実はこのアプローチ、業界内では「次の大波」って見られてるんだ。Lumaiだけじゃなく、新興企業が光演算にシフトしてるのは、NVIDIAも監視してるぐらい。

2026年いま、データセンターの電力危機が本当に深刻化してる。そこに光が答えになるかもしれないってのが、ベンチャーから大手まで考えてることなんだ。

→ 長期投資家は、TSMC製造の半導体だけじゃなく『素材から新しい技術体系』が育つ瞬間も追う価値があるよ。

text:
Let's look at the harsh realities of scaling AI clusters and why the transition to light is mandatory:
​🔴 Copper Interconnects: Overheat and lose severe signal integrity at extreme data rates (800G and 1.6T).
🟢 Silicon Photonics: Uses lasers to transfer data near the speed of light with a fraction of the power consumption.
​With hyperscalers burning billions on electricity, is moving data optically the only way to prevent a total grid collapse?
$ASML $AVGO #semiconductors #ai
(2/4)

text:
THE PHOTONICS ROTATION

Almost nobody is watching photonics.

As AI clusters scale, copper hits physical limits and the next bottleneck becomes optical infrastructure.

Here are 15 names positioned for it:

1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand.

2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity.

4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales.

5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity.

6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand.

7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers.

8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure.

9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules.

10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production.

11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers.

12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient.

13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand.

14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems.

15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster.

Most people won’t care until these are already up 100%.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware are now trading as the sector's most active stocks by dollar volume: trapped-ion systems with major cloud integrations, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and integrated photonics running at room temperature.

This lineup reveals the sheer diversity of bets the market is placing. These aren't just competitors on the same track—they represent fundamentally different architectures, each with its own thesis on how quantum advantage arrives. Cloud-accessible ion traps, purpose-built optimization machines, and portable photonic devices barely share a vocabulary, let alone a roadmap.

That diversity is a sign of a maturing ecosystem. Capital spreading across competing paradigms shows the market is pricing in genuine technical uncertainty, which is the honest position to hold right now. Nobody has a monopoly on the path to broad commercial adoption.

The common thread is that all three are building real access points for enterprise users, whether through cloud marketplaces, developer toolkits, or turnkey hardware. Providers lowering the barrier to experimentation today are positioning themselves to capture demand when quantum workloads scale tomorrow #QuantumComputing

is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. L1: Materials & Substrates The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists. L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics. Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837% InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. L2: Active Photonic Devices This is where light gets made. Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle. Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history. L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds. TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint. Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361% Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP. L4: PIC Platform & IP The brain of the photonic system. Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade. Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte) Top performer: Innolight +948% Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half. L5: Foundry & Process Platform You need a fab. Not just any fab. SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally. Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542% SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026. L6A: Engine & Module Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules. Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely. Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists. L6B: Infrastructure Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster. Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but. Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748% Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic. L6C: Transport Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber. As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down. Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777% WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe L7: Test & Qualification The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping. Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer. Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954% Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives. Built with RTP (Rubidium Titanyl Phosphate) crystal technology, this Q-switch delivers:
🔹 High damage threshold
🔹 Fast switching speeds
🔹 Excellent thermal stability
🔹 No piezoelectric ringing

text:
$AXTI AXT Inc just announced a public offering — size and price not disclosed yet.
Proceeds go toward expanding indium phosphide substrate capacity at its China subsidiary Beijing Tongmei for global export.
InP is critical for data center silicon photonics and 5G infrastructure. Real demand story.
But the catch: AXT has been battling China export permit delays on InP shipments. This raise is to scale capacity that is still export-restricted.
Q1 2026 earnings drop April 30. Dilution risk near-term.
Not financial advice.

text:
Optical communication is rapidly evolving, enabling faster and more efficient global data transmission. Innovations in photonics, fibre optics, and wavelength multiplexing are driving unprecedented bandwidth and reliability. Engineers at Hamamatsu Photonics are advancing high-sensitivity photodiodes and semiconductor laser technologies that underpin modern telecom and data centre infrastructure. These breakthroughs reduce signal loss, increase speed, and support terabit-scale networks across submarine cables and cloud systems. 

As global demand for real-time data grows, optical systems are redefining connectivity with higher efficiency and lower energy use. 

Learn more: https://t.co/osOJovJNha

#engineering #technology #optics #photonics 

@HamamatsuPhoton

text:
The Call for Presentations is still open for the 2026 OCP APAC Summit, taking place August 11 to 12 in Taiwan.

From AI infrastructure and advanced packaging to cooling, photonics, and open data centers, this is where the industry comes together to share what’s next.

This year’s program spans 11 key areas, including AI Clusters, Networking, Photonics, Chiplets & Advanced Packaging, and more.

Submit your proposal by May 18 and be part of the conversations shaping the future of open compute.

Learn more: https://t.co/lihu2mnu9k Registration is now open.

Thank you to our Selection Committee, which includes leaders from across the ecosystem who will guide speaker and content selection.

@OpenComputePrj
#OCPAPAC26 #OCP

That's the photonics company making laser components for directed energy systems. Different kind of conviction.

text:
i keep getting $AXTI vibes from it.

What does $POET tech do?
Photonics is nextgen.  $AXTI proves this.  Placing the laser is labor intensive/expensive/time consuming manual process.  $POET integrates photonics into a chip module.

WHY BUY?
1) needed for next gen AI hardware.
$AMZN - AI chips hitting ceiling - $POET solves this
$MSFT - CHIP production wall - $POET solves this
$META - favors Open Compute standards.  Arista is leader in this and works w/ $POET.  $META + $POET partnership inevitable

$TSLA - investing in DataCenter + Chips.  Will need $POET to get to next gen status

$AVGO & $NVDA - logical that one will buy $POET to prevent competitors from having nextgen bottleneck.

2) low fear dilution - cash runway 8 years

3) moat - 60 patents - years for competitor to replicate

4) no competitor

Tidbit
$AXTI got 4 straight days of gap ups.  UNICORN RARE.  $POET got 3 straight days.

PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second. 2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs. 3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint. 4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028. 5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins. 6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp. 7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "698275", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2047051196981223802", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "154022", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Marvell $MRVL just announced it has agreed to acquire Polariton Technologies, 

"a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices"

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$MRVL ... Marvell to acquire Polariton Technologies to advance next-gen silicon photonics and optical interconnects; financial terms not disclosed (157.32+6.01)
MRVL announced the acquisition of Polariton Technologies, a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices. The acquisition strengthens Marvell's optical technology portfolio by adding advanced modulation capabilities that enable continued scaling in bandwidth, power efficiency, and integration for next-generation coherent and optical interconnect platforms.

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "29479", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2048514663634108784", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "191", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Bottlenecks are the theme of 2026.

We’ve already had:

→ memory bottlenecks
→ photonics bottlenecks
→ CPU bottlenecks
→ GPU bottlenecks

But the next question is bigger:
What powers it all?

AI data centres don’t just need chips.
They need electricity at insane scale.

My power watchlist:

→ $CEG for nuclear
→ $VST for power generation
→ $TLN for nuclear/data centre deals
→ $GEV for turbines + grid equipment
→ $ETN for electrical infrastructure
→ $PWR for grid buildout
→ $OKLO / $SMR for SMRs

The next AI bottleneck will not be inside the data centre.
It will be the power needed to run it.

Don’t miss this one. A pullback soon back could happen. 

This looks a lot like buying $COHR before photonics became a core AI data center bottleneck theme.

AI photonics new age is here. If this sticks, this one could be huge. 

Revenue growth Q: +594%
Gross profit growth Q: +619%
~$69M current assets
~$66M net cash position
~8450 PS ratio 

(reflects extremely early revenue base, not mature scaling)

Still in commercialization / validation phase for EO polymer photonics

This is like buying $COHR at 15$ in 2014

All at a 2B market cap. 

We saw it with $LITE and $COHR — optical infrastructure compounds hard once AI data center demand shifts from narrative to structural bottleneck.

That’s the same structural lane where photonics becomes the invisible layer of compute scaling.

Not saying $LWLG becomes $COHR.

This is where those asymmetries live.

EARLY PHOTONICS INFRASTRUCTURE WORTH BEING ON RADAR 👀

text:
First time I see someone on my feed recommending $AIP. It's nice to see different stocks than the traditional photonics / 800G plays that everybody hypes every day.

I'm also quite bullish on $AIP, although I sold my shares for a quick profit. What I really like is that they are actively decelerating their China business. It accounted for 25-30% before, which was a massive geopolitical headwind.

The downside for me is the royalty timeline. Those revenues only materialize a couple of years after the licensing deal is signed. So while I really like the stock, I'm not sure if it is the best timing to hold right now, but we'll see. The market reflects future potential, not current value so very possible it could go higher in the near term propulsed by INTC and AMD

text:
It's highly nuanced, and I'll explain why it's not late, but late to some: 

Photonics is the newest supercycle (maybe H1 into H2 2025 was the start).

Then there's many different architectural changes in each supercycle:

-> $LITE, $COHR, Innolight, $AXTI and these names led the first 

I did a thesis post on mentioning all four of them as the largest beneficiaries (all are up 500-1000% 1Y)

-> $AAOI, $JBL and others types of names are benefit immensely as the transitional bridge (eg. 1.6T pluggable) 

-> $SIVE, Celestial, Ayar, $POET are others future gens eg. CPO (what I'm focusing on now)

-> VisEra, QD Laser, $ALMU and others are likely going to be future gens (quantum dot, different packaging types, etc) if you fast forward 4 years. 

Of course, $LITE does everything. $AXTI will be used for everything. 

But the amount of pure play exposure for each architectural shift in each mini supercycle is different. 

For example, inp usage with quantum dot is still there, but less used. Or DFB laser arrays for CPO instead of EML. 

There's probably still 50%+ with $LITE and $COHR. And you're a little on the "late" side of things. 

But you're extremely early to new architecture generations. 

What I'm trying to do is point regular retail investors into the direction of new gold mines for free. 

Before institutions figure out sooner or later by paying $20k for equity research reports.

text:
Copper Wall Account Update 🚀

Launched this account last Monday (4/20) — already +4% in under a week.
$5/week DCA into my AI/photonics basket:

$MRVL (data center semis & networking)
$AVGO (AI networking & semiconductors)
$FN (optical component manufacturing)
$CRDO (high-speed connectivity for AI)
$TSEM (specialty analog foundry)
$ARM (CPU architecture powering AI chips)
$ALAB (AI/cloud connectivity solutions)
$TSM (world’s leading chip foundry)
$LITE (optical & photonic components)
$AAOI (fiber-optic networking products)
$HON (industrial tech & automation)
$IONQ (trapped-ion quantum computing)

Thesis: Photonics could be the next major unlock in data processing, and we’re only in Year 3 of a 10-year AI buildout.

This account should look strong by 2032. 

Bullish? 💡📈

text:
THE PHOTONICS ROTATION

Almost nobody is watching photonics.

As AI clusters scale, copper hits physical limits and the next bottleneck becomes optical infrastructure.

Here are 15 names positioned for it:

1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand.

2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity.

4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales.

5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity.

6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand.

7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers.

8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure.

9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules.

10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production.

11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers.

12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient.

13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand.

14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems.

15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster.

Most people won’t care until these are already up 100%.

text:
gabz_investing This is aleabitoreddit (AI/semi supply chain analyst) listing the next key bottlenecks & investment opportunities in the AI data center boom he's tracking:

- **CPO / 1.6T**: Co-Packaged Optics at 1.6 Terabits/sec – next-gen high-speed photonics for AI cluster interconnects (pushing beyond current 800G).

- **Substrates (Glass Core, InP, SoI)**: Advanced materials for chip packaging & silicon photonics (glass core for density/thermal performance; InP/SOI for optical integration).

- **CPUs / ASICs**: Next-wave custom AI processors.

- **Transformers**: Power transformers/switchgear for massive data center electricity demand (major backlog & shortages ahead).

He's frontrunning the photonics + power supply chain supercycle.
@baillat_bruno Great company, but it’s TGV (glass substrate), not photonics.
@ParadisLabs What do you think about $XFAB?? For me it seems that it is a smaller and undiscovered $TSEM equivalent with relatively new asset base to benefit from this photonics cycle

text:
Global Foundries photonics/CPO ecosystem list:

1. $GFS - $30.5B
2. $CDNS - $85.9B
3. $SEIGY - $217.2B
4. $SNPS - $84B
5. $KEYS - $57.3B
6. $ATEYY - $130.4B
7.  RoboTechnik (ficonTEC) - $11.4B
8. $GLW - $141.2B
9. $LITE - $63.8B
10. $SIVE - $950m
11. $FN - $24.7B
12. $ASX - $63.3B

For publicly traded names.

Equal weighted long on the ecosystem from their presentation might not be a bad idea? 

I still find it funny how everything publicly listed is trading in the tens of billions.

Then there's some small Swedish laser company there next to $LITE.
